title: Introduction to Photosynthesis
learning_goals: Explain how plants turn light energy into stored chemical energy.
Describe the roles of chlorophyll, glucose, carbon dioxide, and water.

segments.title: What plants need
segments.content: Plants need sunlight, water, and carbon dioxide. They use these ingredients to make food.

segments.title: The job of chlorophyll
segments.content: Chlorophyll is the green pigment in leaves that absorbs energy from sunlight.

segments.title: Photosynthesis all at once
segments.content: Chlorophyll captures photons, water splits, oxygen leaves, ATP carries energy, carbon fixation uses carbon dioxide, and glucose stores the energy for later. These light reactions and chemical equation describe several connected steps at the same time.

segments.title: The chemical equation
segments.content: 6CO2 plus 6H2O becomes C6H12O6 plus 6O2. This equation summarizes photosynthesis.

segments.title: Why light matters
segments.content: Less sunlight means less energy is available to make glucose.

segments.title: Summary
segments.content: Photosynthesis helps plants make sugar using energy from light.
